_role: Phase 5 progress guard. WASB swap is justified iff its sa_bounce_recall on sa_point_6 (where TrackNetV2 finds zero balls per docs/_investigation/may07_sa_point6_gap.md) is materially > 0, AND its detection_rate inside the 73.2s coverage gap is materially > TrackNetV2's.
_notes: Hand-bootstrapped 2026-05-21. Same source video as a798eff0. SA point 6 frames and stroke spacings from docs/_investigation/may07_sa_point6_gap.md §SA point 6 window. Coverage gap from §Match-wide ball-detection gap pattern. sa_bounce_frames are the 9 SA point 6 strokes — the recall denominator that the +9pp F1 claim must beat. The gap window has no recall anchors (it's a coverage void by definition); detection_rate there is the diagnostic for raw frame coverage in gaps.

windows.name: sa_point_6
windows._note: TrackNetV2 emits zero balls here on production bronze — the WASB hypothesis is that it finds some.

windows.name: gap_73s_slice
windows._note: 400-frame slice from inside the 73.2s coverage gap [899, 2728]. No SA strokes (it's an off-court interval), so sa_bounce_recall has no anchors here — measures raw detection rate only.
